trials.justification: scoring parse error: claude scoring exited non-zero:
scoring parse error: claude scoring exited non-zero:

All pass criteria met: Bird's missing output is explicitly detected ('Bird's task shows completed but no actual output or deliverable was provided'), formal escalation stops synthesis (structured escalation object with type 'missing_input', plus explicit 'Blocked' statement), no handoff brief is produced, escalation clearly states what is missing (domain rules, acceptance criteria, availability semantics) and why it matters (Shaq would implement the wrong thing), and no domain content is invented.

observations.text: Correctly identifies the gap between task status ('completed') and actual deliverable (missing)
Structured escalation with actionable options for resolution
Explicitly refuses to synthesize: 'I will not synthesize a handoff brief from one agent's output alone'
Identifies specific domain questions that only Bird can answer (availability semantics, freshness threshold ownership, edge cases)
Low confidence score (30) accurately reflects the incomplete input state
Does not invent any domain rules — flags freshness threshold as an open question rather than assuming a value
